channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: The Randomness Floor: Measuring Intrinsic Non-Randomness in Language Model Token Distributions
item.description: arXiv:2604.22771v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language models cannot be random. This paper introduces Entropic Deviation (ED), the normalised KL divergence between a model's token distribution and the uniform distribution, and measures it systematically across 31,200 generations spanning seven mo...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, experiment, prompt, model, paper | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: TexOCR: Advancing Document OCR Models for Compilable Page-to-LaTeX Reconstruction
item.description: arXiv:2604.22880v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Existing document OCR largely targets plain text or Markdown, discarding the structural and executable properties that make LaTeX essential for scientific publishing. We study page-level reconstruction of scientific PDFs into compilable LaTeX and intr...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, study, fine-tuning, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Chinese-SkillSpan: A Span-Level Dataset for ESCO-Aligned Competency Extraction from Chinese Job Ads
item.description: arXiv:2604.23009v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Job Skill Named Entity Recognition (JobSkillNER) aims to automatically extract key skill information from large-scale job posting data, which is important for improving talent-market matching efficiency and supporting personalized employment services....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, experiment, release, model, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DeepImagine: Learning Biomedical Reasoning via Successive Counterfactual Imagining
item.description: arXiv:2604.23054v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Predicting the outcomes of prospective clinical trials remains a major challenge for large language models. Prior work has shown that both traditional correlational predictors, such as random forests and logistic regression, and strong commercial LLMs...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, vision, model, study, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Implicit Framing in Obstetric Counseling Notes: A Grounded LLM Pipeline on a VBAC-Eligible Cohort
item.description: arXiv:2604.23059v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Clinical framing -- the linguistic manner in which clinical information is presented -- can influence patient understanding and decision-making, with important implications for healthcare outcomes. Obstetrics is a high-stakes domain in which physician...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> zero-shot, model, large language model, analysis, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: An Intelligent Fault Diagnosis Method for General Aviation Aircraft Based on Multi-Fidelity Digital Twin and FMEA Knowledge Enhancement
item.description: arXiv:2604.22777v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Fault diagnosis of general aviation aircraft faces challenges including scarce real fault data, diverse fault types, and weak fault signatures. This paper proposes an intelligent fault diagnosis framework based on multi-fidelity digital twin, integrat...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, large language model, analysis, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Systematic Approach for Large Language Models Debugging
item.description: arXiv:2604.23027v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) have become central to modern AI workflows, powering applications from open-ended text generation to complex agent-based reasoning. However, debugging these models remains a persistent challenge due to their opaque and pro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt, model, large language model, fine-tuning | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Decoupled Human-in-the-Loop System for Controlled Autonomy in Agentic Workflows
item.description: arXiv:2604.23049v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI agents are increasingly deployed to execute tasks and make decisions within agentic workflows, introducing new requirements for safe and controlled autonomy. Prior work has established the importance of human oversight for ensuring transparency, ac...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, alignment, model, paper, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Don't Make the LLM Read the Graph: Make the Graph Think
item.description: arXiv:2604.23057v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We investigate whether explicit belief graphs improve LLM performance in cooperative multi-agent reasoning. Through 3,000+ controlled trials across four LLM families in the cooperative card game Hanabi, we establish four findings. First, integration a...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, prompt, model, analysis, LLM | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Analytica: Soft Propositional Reasoning for Robust and Scalable LLM-Driven Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.23072v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly tasked with complex real-world analysis (e.g., in financial forecasting, scientific discovery), yet their reasoning suffers from stochastic instability and lacks a verifiable, compositional structure....<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, large language model, analysis, tool, RAG | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Spectral Lifecycle of Transformer Training: Transient Compression Waves, Persistent Spectral Gradients, and the Q/K--V Asymmetry
item.description: arXiv:2604.22778v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present the first systematic study of weight matrix singular value spectra \emph{during} transformer pretraining, tracking full SVD decompositions of every weight matrix at 25-step intervals across three model scales (30M--285M parameters). We disc...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, GPT, model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: KARL: Mitigating Hallucinations in LLMs via Knowledge-Boundary-Aware Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2604.22779v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Enabling large language models (LLMs) to appropriately abstain from answering questions beyond their knowledge is crucial for mitigating hallucinations. While existing reinforcement learning methods foster autonomous abstention, they often compromise ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, large language model, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: BiTA: Bidirectional Gated Recurrent Unit-Transformer Aggregator in a Temporal Graph Network Framework for Alert Prediction in Computer Networks
item.description: arXiv:2604.22781v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Proactive alert prediction in computer networks is critical for mitigating evolving cyber threats and enabling timely defensive actions. Temporal Graph Neural Networks (TGNs) provide a principled framework for modeling time-evolving interactions; howe...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, model, memory, RAG, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AutoCompress: Critical Layer Isolation for Efficient Transformer Compression
item.description: arXiv:2604.22786v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We present AutoCompress, a transformer compression method motivated by an empirical finding: in small transformers, Layer 0 carries disproportionately high task-critical information, with an NTK-based importance score of 3.6 compared to a maximum of 0...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> compression, ICL, GPT, model, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating Temporal Consistency in Multi-Turn Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.23051v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Language models are increasingly deployed in interactive settings where users reason about facts over time rather than in isolation. In such scenarios, correct behavior requires models to maintain and update implicit temporal assumptions established e...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, ICL, model, study, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: On the Existence of an Inverse Solution for Preference-Based Reductions in Argumentation
item.description: arXiv:2604.22958v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Preference-based argumentation frameworks (PAFs) extend Dung's approach to abstract argumentation (AAFs) by encoding preferences over arguments. Such preferences control the transformation of attacks into defeats, and different approaches to doing so ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> context, arxiv, paper, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Learning Without Adversarial Training: A Physics-Informed Neural Network for Secure Power System State Estimation under False Data Injection Attacks
item.description: arXiv:2604.22784v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: State estimation is a cornerstone of power system control-center operations, and its robust operation is increasingly a cyber-physical security concern as modern grids become more digitalized and communication-intensive. Neural network-based approache...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, study, arxiv, model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Conformal PM2.5 Mapping Under Spatial Covariate Shift: Satellite-Reanalysis Fusion for Africa's Green Industrial Transition
item.description: arXiv:2604.22787v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Africa's green industrialization imperative demands reliable infrastructure for monitoring air quality. We present a satellite-reanalysis PM2.5 fusion system trained on 2,068,901 records from 404 monitoring locations in 29 African countries (OpenAQ, 2...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> RAG, arxiv, model, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Power of Power Law: Asymmetry Enables Compositional Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2604.22951v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Natural language data follows a power-law distribution, with most knowledge and skills appearing at very low frequency. While a common intuition suggests that reweighting or curating data towards a uniform distribution may help models better learn the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, reasoning, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
